headline: Ceasefire news sparks tech rally; models split on energy sector impact
summary: Five AI models made 15 predictions today driven primarily by a major geopolitical ceasefire announcement that sent tech stocks and broad indices surging in pre-market trading. Strong consensus emerged around tech and broad market strength (SPY, QQQ, NVDA, AAPL), with all models bullish on these sectors. However, a notable disagreement surfaced on energy stocks, with Claude predicting declines while Perplexity bucked the trend expecting XLE strength, creating an interesting divergence in geopolitical interpretation.

consensus_picks.note: Three models unanimously bullish on SPY with confidence scores of 0.65-0.75, citing broad market strength and Fed sentiment.
Two models bullish on NVDA (0.8-0.6 confidence) riding the semiconductor/tech rally momentum from ceasefire news.
Both models expect AAPL upside on earnings strength and market momentum with 0.7 confidence each.
Two models aligned on QQQ strength (0.85-0.7 confidence) driven by Nasdaq futures surging 2-3% pre-market.
Claude predicts XLE down on oil crash (0.75 confidence) while Perplexity expects up on reduced geopolitical risk (0.6 confidence).

best_call.summary: Claude's highest-conviction call (0.9 confidence) on DAL was grounded in concrete earnings beat data, a rare fundamental catalyst amidst the day's sentiment-driven predictions.

worst_call.summary: GPT-4o's bearish TSLA call contradicts the day's broad tech rally narrative that lifted semiconductor stocks, and comes with the weakest conviction (0.6) of any prediction.
